items.title: Mike Myers, Hazel Mae among recipients of special 2026 Canadian Screen Awards honours
items.summary_auto: Canadian Screen Week takes place in Toronto from May 27 to May 31, culminating with the Canadian Screen Awards ceremony honouring the best in the country's cinema, television and digital media.

items.title: Sask. company says it developed the largest, fastest film scanner on market
items.summary_auto: The company's owner, Greg Miller, developed the scanner through careful research and with the help of a German engineer he found online.

items.title: Polar bears aren't hunting people, they're just out of ice: Scientists
items.summary_auto: "Nutritional stress does play a role. It's just that it doesn't appear to be the role that we thought it was, just driving bears en masse desperately into communities," said Douglas Clark, a professor in the University of Saskatchewan’s School of Environment and Sustainability.

items.title: Grade 9 student attacked by masked intruders at Nova Scotia high school
items.summary_auto: A family is speaking out after two masked intruders entered a Halifax-area high school and violently attacked a Grade 9 student, prompting concerns about school safety and security.

items.title: N.S. bill cloaks identity of children who die in care from public
items.summary_auto: A new bill before the Nova Scotia Legislature is making changes to the way publication bans guard the identity of a child in the care of the province — keeping them in place even if that child dies.

items.title: N.S. opposition leaders renew call to ban NDAs in cases of sexualized violence
items.summary_auto: Opposition leaders say there is no reason for the provincial government's inaction on banning the use of non-disclosure agreements in cases of sexualized violence, abuse and harassment.

items.title: He holds the record for most days skied at Mont Tremblant — and uses status to support others
items.summary_auto: Ken Bull holds the overall record for most days skied in a season at Mont Tremblant set in 2014. He was at the top of the leaderboard for last season as well until a recent re-count placed him in third. He uses his celebrity to update the community on a local skier who suffered a devastating accident in training

items.title: Time running out in Canada for man who caused Humboldt Broncos bus crash
items.summary_auto: Jaskirat Singh Sidhu, the man responsible for the 2018 Humboldt Broncos bus crash, could be deported to India within weeks, leaving behind a wife and two children who are pleading with Canada to let him stay. At the same time, some families of the victims say Sidhu should have been removed from Canada years ago.

items.title: B.C. is moving to permanent daylight time. Could your province be next?
items.summary_auto: British Columbia’s move to permanent daylight time has reignited conversations across the country about scrapping biannual time changes. After B.C. Premier David Eby announced Monday that most of the province will change its clocks for the last time this weekend, Alberta Premier Danielle Smith said it’s time to consider following suit. Other provinces say discussions are happening.

items.title: Family seeking $1.3M owed by Alberta separatist leader Dennis Modry after court order
items.summary_auto: Relatives of a prominent Alberta separatist who met with U.S. officials are upset that he has failed to pay back more than $1.3 million that he owes from a court order issued almost a year ago. A B.C. Supreme Court judge ruled in March 2025 that Dennis Modry, a co-founder of the Alberta Prosperity Project, misappropriated the money from the joint bank account of his aunt and uncle.

items.title: How long can Iran keep firing missiles and drones?
items.summary_auto: Experts say Tehran will likely try to inflict collateral damage in neighbouring countries and exhaust U.S. and Israeli defences by using cheap kamikaze drones in order to try and save the regime.

items.title: U.S. could hold a 'red card' over Canada's Gripen fighter jet option
items.summary_auto: As Ottawa reviews its F-35 purchase, there's concern Washington holds a powerful lever — control of the secure Link 16 network used for NATO and NORAD operations. Swedish Gripens and Danish F-35s operate together in NATO missions, but U.S. approval is required for the encryption system — raising concerns it could be withheld if Canada chooses the Gripen.

items.title: Months after suspension for racist posts, Hamilton officer's car bears sticker with symbol ‘appropriated by’ neo-Nazis
items.summary_auto: A Hamilton community group is calling for action after it says a resident discovered a police officer's vehicle is once again displaying a sticker “associated with white nationalist extremism." Police had recently investigated the officer for sharing racist and extremist content on social media.

items.title: As Iran war raises terror risk, U.S. enforcement agencies are spread thin, experts say
items.summary_auto: After a year of upheaval, departures and shifting priorities at some of the U.S. agencies charged with keeping the U.S. safe, experts say the U.S. may be more vulnerable to a terror attack. The conflict with Iran also comes as hundreds of Homeland Security personnel are not being paid or have been furloughed because of the government shutdown.

items.title: Why researchers say lowering Canada's radon limit could prevent more cases of lung cancer and save lives
items.summary_auto: Could a lower threshold for radon levels in Canadian homes protect more people from getting lung cancer? Some researchers say Canada is overdue for an evidence-backed rethink in light of emerging research and rising risks.

items.title: 'It's not easy, let's be clear': Carney speaks frankly about dealing with Trump at an event in Australia
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney pulled back the curtain on his relationship with his American counterpart Wednesday evening in Australia saying it's not easy dealing with U.S. President Donald Trump and he must choose his words carefully to avoid any blowback.

items.title: Manitoba woman killed in Dominican Republic bus crash remembered as 'fearless' adventurer
items.summary_auto: A Manitoba woman who died after a bus accident in the Dominican Republic on Sunday is being remembered as a "fearless" traveller who was passionate about exploring the world.

items.title: Who will lead Iran now that Khamenei is gone? It's not clear
items.summary_auto: In the days since the surprise death of Iran's supreme leader at the hands of the U.S. and Israel Saturday, there has been much speculation — and confusion — about who's in charge and who will succeed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: NHL commissioner says criticism of U.S. men's hockey team at Olympics 'unfortunate'
items.summary_auto: NHL commissioner Gary Bettman declined an opportunity Tuesday to weigh in on the political aspect of the U.S. hockey celebration at the Milan-Cortina Olympics but did say he thought what was portrayed "was unfortunate and not accurate."

items.title: Bettman says criticism of U.S. men's hockey team at Olympics 'unfortunate'
items.summary_auto: NHL commissioner Gary Bettman declined an opportunity Tuesday to weigh in on the political aspect of the U.S. hockey celebration at the Milan-Cortina Olympics but did say he thought what was portrayed "was unfortunate and not accurate."

items.title: Convicted fraudster who admitted he 'destroyed families' pleads guilty mid-trial
items.summary_auto: A few years after he was released from prison after serving more than five years for fraud, Scott Brooks began targeting victims once again, a judge heard Tuesday as Brooks pleaded guilty mid-trial to six counts of fraud.

items.title: Israel sends troops to southern Lebanon, as Hezbollah vows it's ready for 'open war'
items.summary_auto: Israel sent troops into southern Lebanon on Tuesday and warned residents of more than 80 villages to evacuate as the Iran-backed militant Hezbollah group said it was ready for an "open war" with Israel in the wake of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: How Iran is strangling the world's oil supply | About That
items.summary_auto: Iran effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, threatening to attack any ship trying to pass through. Andrew Chang explains why the country is able to have such an impact, and to what extent the ripple effects may be felt. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Live Nation's antitrust trial is underway in the U.S. What could it mean for Canada?
items.summary_auto: The antitrust trial between Ticketmaster’s parent company and the U.S. Department of Justice got underway with opening statements on Tuesday. The case could force a breakup of Ticketmaster and Live Nation and have ripple effects in Canada — but experts say it’s still early, and bets on the outcome are off the table.

items.title: Live Nation's antitrust trial is underway in the U.S. What's at stake in the case?
items.summary_auto: The antitrust trial between Ticketmaster’s parent company and the U.S. Department of Justice got underway with opening statements on Tuesday. The case could force a breakup of Ticketmaster and Live Nation and have ripple effects in Canada — but experts say it’s still early, and bets on the outcome are off the table.

items.title: Fans will 'soon' learn if Calgary-Edmonton bid to host 2028 World Cup of Hockey a success
items.summary_auto: Speaking in Calgary on Tuesday, NHL commissioner Gary Bettman says a decision will be made "soon" on who will host the 2028 World Cup of Hockey.

items.title: 3 children dead in winter activity incidents are a reminder of the dangers involved
items.summary_auto: Since early February, at least three children have died in winter-related activities in Quebec alone. The Montreal Children’s Hospital’s trauma director says this is a reminder that activities like sledding should be undertaken with caution and parental supervision.

items.title: Canadians in Persian Gulf say they are living by the grace of missile defence systems
items.summary_auto: Canadian expats and those trying to get back home from the Persian Gulf say they're living under the constant threat of missile and drone strikes but are taking solace in what they describe as highly effective air-defence systems keeping them safe from harm.

items.title: She was about to take her Ottawa business national, when the Spice Girls' lawyers threatened to sue
items.summary_auto: An Ottawa woman who has been selling her custom spice blends since she was 13 says she's being forced to change her company's name after lawyers for the pop band Spice Girls threatened to sue her.

items.title: Carney says his support for U.S. and Israel's war on Iran 'not a blank cheque'
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says his support for U.S President Donald Trump's attack on Iran comes amid a "failure of the international order" and is "not a blank cheque."

items.title: 6 Toronto police officers charged in corruption probe were supposed to appear in court. Why didn’t they?
items.summary_auto: Their absences aren’t actually against the law. But because this is a high-profile case and none of the officers scheduled to appear Tuesday are in custody, questions persist about when they’ll finally make a public appearance.

items.title: Class action lawsuit over migrant farm workers' EI benefits, restrictions to proceed
items.summary_auto: A class action lawsuit alleging Canada's Seasonal Agricultural Worker Program exploits the labour and Charter rights of agricultural workers can proceed, a judge decided last week.

items.title: How Western countries are handling getting citizens out of the Middle East
items.summary_auto: As the effects of the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran spread across the Middle East, major Western countries are scrambling to make arrangements for getting their citizens out of the region.

items.title: After fatal collisions, Northern Ontario NDP MPPs raise highway safety concerns through road trip
items.summary_auto: NDP members of provincial parliament are embarking on a nine-day road trip across northern Ontario to raise awareness about ongoing safety concerns along the Highway 11-17 corridor. Highway safety has been top of mind in the region following a recent string of fatal collisions, where eight deaths were reported in a 10-day period. Here's what we know about the tour, called “Our Roads, Our Safety.”

items.title: Queen's University group stranded in Middle East looking for a way home
items.summary_auto: Sixteen students and their professor from Queen's University are safe in Doha, Qatar, after their plan was turned around mid-air on Feb. 28.

items.title: Fact check: Fake CBC article about Doug Ford
items.summary_auto: Can you spot a fake CBC article? This one features Ontario Premier Doug Ford announcing an investment program. It’s not a real article, but a scam to take your money.

items.title: Kristi Noem stands by remarks accusing U.S. citizens killed in Minneapolis of terrorism
items.summary_auto: U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem is standing by her remarks calling the acts of two U.S. citizens shot dead by federal immigration officers in Minneapolis "domestic terrorism."

items.title: Iran holds funerals for girls, staff killed in air strike on school
items.summary_auto: Thousands of people gathered in Minab, Iran, for mass funerals of victims killed on Saturday when a girl’s elementary school was hit by airstrikes. Iranian media is reporting that 168 children were killed and the UN's High Commissioner for Human Rights is calling for an ‘impartial and thorough’ investigation into the strike.
